body.title: [triage] Inference dispatch turns a 0.03s 'model not found' into a 60s 'timed out' — the true cause was RIGHT THERE and got thrown away
body.intention: I intend that dispatch reports the cause the provider actually gave it — because an error that is REPLACED by a slower, vaguer error is worse than no error handling at all: it costs a minute and then lies about why. [intention_date: 2026-07-13]
body.description:
MEASURED 2026-07-13 while closing T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal. Every component in the path answers in MILLISECONDS. Dispatch takes SIXTY SECONDS and then reports the wrong thing.

**THE EVIDENCE (all measured, this host, minutes apart):**
  * `curl` POST /api/chat model=llama3.2:3b  -> `404 {"error":"model 'llama3.2:3b' not found"}` in **0.011s**
  * the EXACT urllib call device.py makes -> `HTTPError 404 ... not found` in **0.03s**
  * `curl https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/models` -> `200` in **0.27s** (outbound network is fine)
  * `InferenceDevice.dispatch(task_class='minion')` -> **60.2s**, then `socket.timeout('timed out')`

**TWO DEFECTS, AND THE SECOND IS (AGAIN) THE IMPORTANT ONE.**

**(1) THE ROUTE POINTS AT A MODEL THAT IS NOT INSTALLED.** The rules engine resolves `minion -> llama3.2:3b@ollama` (`rule=resolve->llama3.2:3b@ollama (difficulty=classify)`). This host's ollama has THREE models: `qwen2.5:7b`, `llama3.2:1b`, `nomic-embed-text`. **`llama3.2:3b` is not one of them.** The registry asserts a capability the world does not have — and NOTHING checks: HealthMonitor probes SOURCES ('anthropic is DOWN'), never MODELS, so a live source hosting an absent model reads as healthy. **Consequence: the minion tier — the tier every classify task uses — has a 0% success rate on this host, and the rack reports itself healthy.**

**(2) THE TRUE CAUSE IS DISCARDED AND REPLACED BY A WORSE ONE.** Ollama's answer is fast, specific, and TRUE: *model not found*. `_ollama_chat` even handles it correctly (`except HTTPError -> RuntimeError(f'Ollama {code}: {body}')`). Yet what reaches the caller is a bare `socket.timeout` whose entire message is **'timed out'** — sixty seconds later. So the failure that DID happen (a missing model — instantly fixable, `ollama pull`) is indistinguishable from the failure that did NOT (a slow/hung model — a capacity problem). **Two causes, one signal, and the signal names the wrong one.** This is R-feedback-is-unconditional / the non-injective-signal defect AGAIN, one layer below the intent extractor — and it is why the extractor's crash was misread for a month: even now that the extractor honestly records `error_detail='inference: timed out'`, IT IS FAITHFULLY RECORDING A LIE IT WAS TOLD. A device can only be as honest as the layer beneath it.

**THE MECHANISM IS UNKNOWN — DO NOT GUESS IT (CP1).** Both endpoints respond in <0.3s; nothing in the path should take 60s. `req.timeout` defaults to 60, so SOMETHING is blocking on a socket that is not the one that already answered. Candidates NOT yet distinguished: a fallback/escalation hop to a second provider after the ollama 404; a thread/future the RuntimeError never propagates out of (see T-inference-single-device-instance-no-thread-per-call); a health-probe socket; a retry loop. **Find it by instrumenting the crossing, not by reasoning about it** (instrument-don't-probe).

**Affected files:** unseen_university/devices/inference/device.py (dispatch + `_ollama_chat` :830-855 + the OpenRouter path :790-828), rules_engine.py / connections.py (the route to an uninstalled model), the HealthMonitor (probes sources, not models).

**Scope boundary:** IN — (a) make the reported cause the cause the provider GAVE (a 404 must surface as a 404, in 0.03s, not as a 60s timeout); (b) a model the source does not have must fail FAST and SAY SO; (c) find and fix whatever burns the 60 seconds. OUT — choosing a better minion model, and the extractor's own record (done: T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal).

**Test plan / discriminating fixture:** a fake provider that returns HTTP 404 'model not found' and a fake provider that genuinely HANGS must produce **DIFFERENT** errors at the dispatch boundary, and the 404 must return in well under the timeout. A build that merely lengthens or shortens the timeout fails this: the defect is not the DURATION, it is that two causes emit one signal.

**Completion criteria:**
  1. A 404/model-not-found surfaces to the caller with the provider's own message, fast (<1s).
  2. A genuine hang still reports a timeout — and the two are DISTINGUISHABLE in the stored record and the log.
  3. The 60-second black hole is FOUND and named (a number and a stack, not 'it's fixed now').
  4. A source that is up but lacks the routed model does not report healthy.

**BLOCKS:** the live success-rate re-measurement of the intent extractor (criterion 3 of T-intent-extractor-crash-masquerades-as-refusal) — no minion-tier call can succeed on this host until the route reaches an installed model.
